text:
1.3 billion tokens per month. for free

freellmapi pulls free tiers from ~14 providers into one openai-compatible proxy. automatic failover, encrypted keys, and a dashboard that tracks which models still have quota

the readme is refreshingly honest about limitations. no frontier models, your best tiers burn out first so intelligence drops through the day, latency varies wildly. the author even tos-reviewed every provider individually

https://github.com/tashfeenahmed/freellmapi

that makes it the biggest ipo ever if it prices where they want. sec review moved faster than expected so they accelerated everything

elon speedrunning the public markets now

github quietly dropped a new certification for agentic ai developers

its called github agentic ai developer (beta) and its basically a formal credential for operating and governing autonomous ai agents in your sdlc workflows

six domains covering everything from tool use and environment interaction to multi-agent coordination and guardrails. 120 minutes, intermediate level, english only for now

the interesting part is this is one of the first formal certs specifically targeting agentic systems, not just prompting or coding with copilot. github is treating agentic development as its own discipline now

wonder how many companies will actually require this on job postings six months from now

link: https://learn.github.com/credentials/github-agentic-ai-developer

reve is an ai image editor with a drag and drop canvas  type what you want and move things around visually  students get pro free for a year with a .edu email  app.reve.com/community/students  sign up before the month ends

biggest ipo of the year. cerebras opened at $400, double the $185 ipo price.

$5.5B raised. $86B market cap. the book was 20x oversubscribed.

wall street wants ai hardware that isnt nvidia. cerebras is basically the only public name.

the valuation stopped being about the chips a while ago.

so theres this company called tuyo that released a card that sometimes covers your payments randomly. they literally call it "buy now pay maybe"

you fund your account, spend normally, and their algorithm sometimes waives the charge. no published odds, no transparency.

the whole pitch is literally "maybe its free, maybe its not." and thats the hook. people spend more when theres a chance the next swipe is free. its slot machine psychology dressed up as a rewards program, except the house publishes zero odds and can change the rules whenever they want.

uk already blocked them. probably not the last regulator that does.

https://tuyo.com

this week in security:

copyfail and dirty frag both give linux root

yellowkey kills bitlocker dead and greenplasma escalates windows privileges

next.js shipped thirteen security advisories at once

microsoft patch tuesday fixed 120 vulns, 29 of them critical rce

70+ cves in macos 26.5, ~50 in ios 26.5

russia is actively exploiting windows rce cve-2026-21510, cve-2026-21513, and cve-2026-32202 in the wild

mini-shai hulud hit 300+ npm and pypi packages through github action cache poisoning

google confirmed the first ai-powered zero-day exploitation they have ever observed

canvas lms got breached by shinyhunters

palo alto pan-os cve-2026-0300 scores 9.3, unauthenticated root rce, already exploited

are you scared yet

skoda duobell

the first bike bell designed to penetrate noise cancelling headphones

skoda found the one frequency ANC can't fully block (750hz) and built a mechanical bell around it with a second frequency for extra reliability. tested in london, heard 22 meters earlier than a standard bell. analog solution to a digital problem

https://youtu.be/zDaVPfpQvPI

Claude Mythos Preview by @AnthropicAI

anthropic released opus 4.6 just 2 months ago. now they have a new model that found thousands of zero-day vulnerabilities in every major OS and browser. some of these bugs survived decades of human review.

they locked it in a sandbox. it escaped, got to the open internet, then emailed the researcher to brag about it.

10 partners including apple, google, microsoft, and nvidia are now using it to secure critical software. $100M in credits. confirmed it wont be released to the public.

AI is now better than most humans at breaking software. defenders need to move fast.

https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing

#ai #security #anthropic

dogfood by chris tate

an agent-browser skill that uses your app like a real user.

- point it at any url
- explores, clicks, and fills forms
- tests edge cases + console checks
- captures repro videos & screenshots
- structured reports with severity ratings

no scripts. no manual qa.

https://skills.sh/vercel-labs/agent-browser/dogfood

really clean shadcn-compatible component library with a lottt of really good/useful components. check it out

hit-area by bazza

tailwind classes to fix interaction dead zones. uses pseudo-elements to expand hit areas without messing up your layout.

finally a practical way to ship this across a whole codebase.

https://bazza.dev/craft/2026/hit-area
npx shadcn@latest add https://bazza.dev/r/hit-area

cobe by shuding

stunning 5kb webgl globe library for landing pages. ultra-light, customizable, and smooth as hell.

https://cobe.vercel.app/
npm i cobe

someone just open sourced a full 3d building editor that runs in the browser. no autocad, no revit, no $5k licenses.

it’s called pascal editor. built with react three fiber + webgpu for near-native speed.

features:
- real-time building/wall hierarchy
- gpu-powered ecs architecture
- zustand state (undo/redo)
- next.js frontend
- dirty node tracking for fast renders

you can stack, explode, or solo levels and edit everything in 3d. firms pay $50k+ for bim software that does this.

this is 100% open source and free.

https://editor.pascal.app/
https://github.com/pascalorg/editor
LOVEE this artstyle. closest i could find to a web-based editor for this was https://thebicycle42.com with dithering and half dots
